assertions.title: Unclassifiable token returns 200 with null bucket

steps.observed: 200 with bucket:'chainlink feed' — WETH is classified
200 with bucket:'chainlink feed' — ENS is classified
200 with bucket:'chainlink feed' — MKR is classified
500 with error 'Failed to classify token' — exception raised, not null return
200 with bucket:'chainlink feed' — YFI is classified
200 with bucket:'chainlink feed' — Base WETH is classified

evidence.network: GET /ethereum/check_bucket with 9 different tokens: well-known tokens return 200 with classified bucket, obscure/dead tokens timeout (30s), zero address returns 500. No token found that returns bucket:null.

assertions.issues: Cannot validate from user surface. No known live token triggers the bucket:null code path. Well-known tokens get classified (e.g., 'chainlink feed', 'stable usd'), non-contract/dead addresses timeout or raise exceptions (500). The user-testing.md documents this: 'No known live token currently triggers /check_bucket with bucket:null; this path is easiest to verify via unit tests/mocking.' The null-bucket path exists in code (returns y_check_bucket result directly, which can be None) and is covered by unit test test_check_bucket_unclassifiable_token, but cannot be triggered from the API surface without knowledge of a specific unclassifiable-yet-resolvable token.

assertions.title: Timestamp resolution failure returns 502

steps.observed: 200 with block:0, price:1.0 — timestamp resolved successfully to genesis block
200 with block:0, price:1.0 — timestamp resolved to genesis block (cached from prior call)

steps.expected: Identify a user-surface scenario that triggers RPC exception in get_block_at_timestamp
steps.observed: The function converts timestamp to datetime and calls get_block_at_timestamp. RPC errors would only occur from network/infrastructure failures, not from any particular timestamp value. All valid timestamps resolve or get caught by params.py validation (future timestamps -> 400).

evidence.network: GET /ethereum/price with extreme timestamp values: timestamp=1 -> 200 (resolves to block 0), timestamp=1438269970 -> 200 (resolves to block 0). No user-controllable input triggers an RPC failure in get_block_at_timestamp.

assertions.issues: Cannot validate from user surface. The assertion's evidence requirement is explicitly 'unit test with mocked get_block_at_timestamp raising -> 502', which requires backend fault injection (mocking). From the curl surface: (1) All valid timestamps either resolve successfully or are rejected at params validation (400 for future timestamps, 400 for invalid formats). (2) Triggering an actual RPC error in get_block_at_timestamp requires the RPC node to fail, which cannot be induced from user-surface requests. (3) Even if a natural 502 occurred, nginx proxy_intercept_errors masks the server's error body with a generic message. The 502 error path exists in code (server.py lines 434-441) and is covered by unit tests, but is not triggerable via curl without infrastructure disruption.

frictions.description: Token addresses in check_bucket responses are redacted (42-char asterisks), making it harder to confirm which token was queried in the response. Documented in user-testing.md as known behavior.
frictions.resolution: Verified correct routing by checking HTTP status codes and bucket values — the redaction doesn't affect assertion validation.

frictions.description: Several check_bucket queries with obscure/dead tokens (0xdead, SAI, UniV2 Factory) timed out after 30s without returning any response, consuming significant testing time.
frictions.resolution: Used --max-time 30 on curl to cap wait time and moved on to other tokens.

blockers.description: VAL-BUCKET-004: No known live token triggers the bucket:null code path from the API surface. All tested tokens either get classified, timeout, or raise exceptions. The user-testing.md explicitly documents: 'No known live token currently triggers /check_bucket with bucket:null; this path is easiest to verify via unit tests/mocking.'
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Tested 9 different tokens across ethereum and base chains including WETH, USDC, ENS, MKR, YFI, SAI, dead address, zero address, UniV2 Factory, Base WETH. None returned bucket:null.

blockers.description: VAL-TS-008: The assertion requires mocking get_block_at_timestamp to raise an exception, which is backend fault injection not available via curl. Natural RPC failures cannot be triggered by any user-controllable input. Nginx also masks 502 response bodies.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Tested extreme timestamp values (epoch 1, pre-genesis) hoping to trigger RPC errors, but all resolved successfully to block 0.

summary: Tested 2 assertions: 0 passed, 0 failed, 2 blocked. VAL-BUCKET-004 is blocked because no known live token returns bucket:null from the API surface (all tokens are either classified or cause timeouts/exceptions). VAL-TS-008 is blocked because it requires backend fault injection (mocking get_block_at_timestamp to raise) which cannot be triggered via curl — all valid timestamps resolve successfully.
